title: Fix worker loop: sleep only after execution, not on fixed tick
description: Current loop: sleep(60) runs at the END of every cycle regardless. When a task executes for 90s, the next poll happens only 60s later — giving a window as short as the task duration for races. Fix: move sleep(60) so it only runs AFTER all tasks are processed (or when idle). Specifically: the outer 'while true' loop currently does: git_sync → find tasks → process_task → sleep. Change to: git_sync → find tasks → if found: process all then sleep(60) → if idle: sleep(60). The sleep must come AFTER process_task completes, not on a parallel tick. Also: do NOT sleep when git_sync fails (already retrying via continue) — keep the existing early-continue behavior. The inner 'while true' task drain loop already processes all available tasks before reaching sleep — verify this is preserved.
